diagrams.title: Architecture of Transformational Grammar
diagrams.description: This diagram shows the overall structure of a transformational grammar system, illustrating how the base component (context-free grammar) generates a deep structure, which then passes through transformational rules to produce a surface structure. This helps visualize the flow from abstract meaning to actual sentence form.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the fundamental architecture of transformational grammars and how context-free rules serve as the foundation for more complex linguistic transformations.

diagrams.title: Context-Free Grammar Parse Tree
diagrams.description: This diagram displays a parse tree (derivation tree) demonstrating how a sentence is recursively decomposed into phrase structure constituents using context-free production rules. Shows the hierarchical structure from Sentence (S) down to terminal symbols (words).
elements: Phrase structure nodes (NP, VP, Det, N, V)
Production rule annotations (S → NP VP, etc.)
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates how context-free grammars parse sentences into hierarchical tree structures, demonstrating the recursive nature of natural language syntax.

diagrams.title: Transformational Rule Example: Active to Passive
diagrams.description: This diagram shows a concrete example of a transformational rule, comparing the deep structure and surface structure of an active voice sentence versus its passive voice equivalent. Demonstrates how transformations manipulate syntactic structure while preserving underlying meaning.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how transformational rules operate on deep structures to produce different surface forms, illustrating the core concept of transformation in transformational grammars.
